mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad-based economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This is a direct measure of aggregate labor demand and economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery shows consistent job growth since 2010.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a canonical leading indicator of recession. This signals a sharp deterioration in the labor market, leading to lower consumer spending, corporate defaults, and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with an accommodative or lagging central bank (stable FEDFUNDS) erodes real corporate profit margins and increases uncertainty, leading to a higher equity risk premium and negative returns.
failure_condition: The specified joint condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals a robust recovery in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, boosting investor confidence and driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current context is recovery from 2008-09 recession.
failure_condition: During periods where UNRATE > 6%, positive momentum in INDPRO is not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative surprise in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an abrupt weakening of consumer demand, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This directly implies lower future corporate revenues and triggers negative short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (predicting a negative 5-day return) is less than 55% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises the FEDFUNDS rate above a neutral level, it actively tightens financial conditions to combat inflation. This increases the discount rate on future earnings and restricts credit, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical tightening cycles (e.g., 2004-2006) often precede market downturns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is above 2.5%, the median 20-day S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than in periods where it is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a key leading indicator of broad economic activity due to their wide-ranging impact on construction, materials, and durable goods consumption. A sustained upward trend signals economic strength and predicts positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market is recovering from the 2008 crisis, providing a tailwind.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 12-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) is the definition of a recession. Even though it is a lagging indicator, a confirmed negative print removes ambiguity about the state of the economy and forces a repricing of risk assets downwards.
failure_condition: Following the release of a negative quarterly GDPC1 print, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.
